column1: # Feature sections — YOU define these. One row per section.
#   - a pattern ending in "/"  matches that directory RECURSIVELY (prefix match)
#   - otherwise standard shell globbing applies (no recursion across "/")
# SECTIONING MODEL = IMPORTANCE RANK (user-supplied; rank 1 = most-used entry point).
# Each export is homed in exactly one rank. Source globs are coarse staleness
# triggers (file -> section), so a file shared across ranks may flag several
# sections — that is expected. BDD depth is highest for rank 1 and tapers down.

column3: Rank 1 (most used) — ptr_app, pp* placeholders, ptr_define_placeholder_*

column2: R/paintr-default-args.R R/paintr-server.R R/paintr-input-spec.R R/paintr-data.R R/paintr-options.R R/paintr-copy.R R/paintr-registry.R R/paintr-render.R R/paintr-eval.R
column3: Rank 5 — everything else (defaults, extractors, options, init/state, utils)
